discussion.p: The Effects element of `vprint_nonunicode_buffered(FILE* stream, string_view fmt, format_args args)` in p14 is specified as:

blockquote.pre: string out = vformat(fmt, args); vprint_nonunicode("{}", make_format_args(out));

discussion.p:
That call selects the two-argument overload `vprint_nonunicode(string_view, format_args)` ( p13), whose Effects element delegates to `vprint_nonunicode(stdout, fmt, args)`. The caller-supplied stream is discarded and the output destination becomes `stdout`.
The parallel function `vprint_unicode_buffered` ( p8) correctly passes `stream`:

blockquote.pre: string out = vformat(fmt, args); vprint_unicode(stream, "{}", make_format_args(out));

discussion.p: This is user-visible: `print(FILE* stream, ...)` ( p2) calls `vprint_nonunicode_buffered(stream, ...)` on the non-UTF-8 buffered path, so the current wording routes output to the wrong stream.

blockquote.pre: void vprint_nonunicode_buffered(FILE* stream, string_view fmt, format_args args);
string out = vformat(fmt, args); vprint_nonunicode(stream, "{}", make_format_args(out));
